properties.name: August Coffee
properties.address: Shop 6/16-22 George St, North Strathfield NSW 2137, Australia

reviews.author: Kris Xue
reviews.text:
Prociutto Pear on Sourdough
The pear was slightly roasted with sugar, very moist and with just the right amount of sweetness. Paired with the salty and fragrant Prociutto, it was unexpectedly delicious. Both of these special drinks （Iced Rose Black Einspenner and Matcha Monster ）are very good. When I ordered, the clerk recommended their latte art to me, saying that the latte art guy in the shop won first place in the Sydney latte art competition. We ordered a labubu and unicorn , and it felt really good and the price was very reasonable

reviews.author: Shabbir Afridi
reviews.text:
Good coffee good iced matcha

Why not 5 stars ??
Because of multiple things with the food.
The bread on mushroom was a white sourdough and hard as rock, required muscles to break it.
A good chef knows how to serve a softer healthier bread. The mushroom and the cheese were deep fried. Makes it a bad tasting combo and  an unhealthy Choice. The only good thing was poached eggs which i can even make at home. Horrendous experience. My friends plate had horrible tasting vegetables. Salmon didn’t go well with the omelette. Overall 0 i won’t be coming here again

reviews.author: Caleb
reviews.text:
Matcha monster while it has 3 types of matcha, it was super sweet. Almost like a matcha milkshake with how sweet and creamy it was. It's honestly perfect for me, but if you're used to a more bitter and earthy tasting matcha this might be too sweet for you.

Pineapple ham & cheese croissant, a banging sweet and salty combo. Never would've thought to use pineapple jam, but it works . Perfect amount of all 3 ingredients which allowed all the flavours to come through. Croissant was slightly crisp, with a very soft interior. I do wish it was slightly more crisp/toasted though.

Most of you should know my stance on prosciutto & pear in a sandwich. This on sourdough was no different. No honey, but the pears had been roasted/caramelised and was extra sweet which made up for no honey. This did have hollandaise sauce which I'm not the biggest fan of, but it added creamy-ness.

They asked me to try something off their up coming menu and not gonna lie, I was very hesitant. But to my surprise, it was freakin amazing. All the flavours worked soooo well and the charred vegetables had like a deli meat (ham) texture which I ain't mad about. Foccacia was different from others with it being soft and white bread like (?), it didn't have that usual chewy interior.

properties.name: Tea Journal
properties.address: Chatswood Interchange, 44/436 Victoria Ave, Sydney NSW 2067, Australia

reviews.author: Life AtBlk43
reviews.text:
Lots of tea options here, which I really like. I have tried the sweet ginger tea and the lemongrass ginger on separate visits, the sweet ginger one is my favorite so far.

I also tried their sesame cake and pistachio cake. Pistachio wins for me with mild but clear flavor, chiffon like texture. But the cakes feel overpriced for what they are. I would rather go to another cafe(like Lode or Salt and Light) if im craving dessert or pastry. I will only come back here for the tea, not the cakes.

The cafe is usually full whenever I drop by, so it does get noisy. To be fair, i have never seen it quiet or half empty, so maybe not the best place for reading or working. But it is a good spot to sit down for tea and catch up with a friend.

Service is good, kudos especially the guy who was working around 4 to 5pm on Dec 16, 2025. He is very attentive and kept refilling my hot water without needing to ask.

Tea ceremony vibes. Social place. I will try their matcha next time.

reviews.author: Diwa Venkata
reviews.text:
I had such a lovely experience at The Tea Journal and it absolutely deserves a 5/5. From the moment I walked in, the ambience stood out — calm, cosy and thoughtfully designed, making it a perfect place to slow down and actually enjoy your tea. It feels welcoming without being noisy, and there’s a gentle, relaxing vibe that makes you want to stay longer than planned.

The highlight for me was the ginger tea with goji berries. It was beautifully balanced — warm, fragrant and naturally comforting without being overpowering. You can really tell care goes into the preparation, and it felt both refreshing and soothing at the same time. If you enjoy tea that feels intentional rather than rushed, this one is a must-try.

I also had the lychee rose cake and it was delicious. Light, delicate and not overly sweet, with a lovely floral note that paired perfectly with the tea. The texture was soft and fresh, and it felt like a thoughtfully crafted dessert rather than something generic.

Service was superb throughout the visit. Staff were attentive, friendly and genuinely welcoming, creating an experience that felt warm and personal without being intrusive. They were happy to explain menu items and made everything feel easy and relaxed.

Overall, this is a standout spot in Chatswood for tea lovers or anyone wanting a peaceful café experience with high-quality offerings. Great drinks, beautiful desserts, wonderful atmosphere and exceptional service — I’ll definitely be coming back. Highly recommended if you want somewhere calm, refined and genuinely enjoyable.

reviews.author: Emma
reviews.text: Lovely spot! Friendly staff and really flavourful iced tea that you can get takeaway or sit in. Lots of teas and beautiful tea cups and tea pots to buy - from dragon pearl green tea, to unique Sichuan bamboo needle tea, and west lake dragon well. Definitely the place to go to buy a special gift for someone who loves tea or a treat for yourself.

reviews.author: Dianne Lim
reviews.text:
Was our first meal from landing. For some reason everything was on the sweet side. Even the mentaiko pasta was on the sweet side - abit like in Korea where even garlic bread is sweet 😂

Salmon ochazuke was also a tad sweet as was our coffees. Tho I did order skim milk so not sure if that had anything to do with it. Was bit disappointing but not terrible all in all. Spent about AUD$63 for 2

reviews.author: Gary Lee
reviews.text:
I think there was some japanese dashi like flavoring in their scrambled? It was a nice touch that kept it from being bland without going overboard with the ingredients like some other breakfast places. Classy.

I also like how they steamed the chocolate into the  foam for the soy mocha. I went with large size with extra shot and it just just right, might have been a bit weak if not for the extra shot.

reviews.author: Jodie Louise
reviews.text: Such a great little coffee spot in Haymarket. Edition Roasters does really good coffee, smooth, well made, and clearly crafted by people who know what they’re doing. The space is cozy and relaxed, perfect for a quick stop or a short sit-down. Staff are friendly and welcoming, which makes it even better. An easy go-to if you’re around the area and want quality coffee without the fuss.

reviews.author: emily park
reviews.text:
Stumbled on this cafe randomly after dinner and turned out to be such a great experience!

The staff were so kind and welcoming from the moment I walked in. There were so many available seats and also had free wifi🙏😻 I also tried one of their cookies with the iced green tea latte and both were genuinely so good. Everything tasted high quality and made with care. This is definitely a spot I’ll be coming back to, highly recommend if you’re looking for great service and something sweet to enjoy in Lidcombe!

reviews.author: Jenny Vo
reviews.text: my go-to study cafe! spacious upstairs with seating aswell which is very convenient. the food and drinks are always yummy! they have a new dubai choc drink that isn’t too sweet with fun crunchy pistachio kataifi - give it a try!

reviews.author: Janet Li
reviews.text: I went to this amazing cafe close to Lidcombe station last week and experienced the best customer service! Yellow Days cafe is also a great place to get some study in. The waitress was very kind and made my iced matcha latte which was pretty good. The bathroom was clean and organised which made my experience even better. I would recommend this place to anyone who wants to have a bite and is in the area! They also have the new dubai chocolate drink which I also ordered for my ride home!

reviews.author: Timothy the explorer
reviews.text:
For me the coffee was good. The mushroom on toast was yummy it had lots of different mushrooms. 😋
The view of watching trains and people was really good because I was enjoying my time on the second level. The service for me was of a good level. To me It had a happy vibe.

reviews.author: Gloria Chan
reviews.text:
I have tried their earl grey cake and the butter scotch latte. Love the latte, the jelly of the earl grey cake is too sweet, but it’s nice once you take that off.
Their pastries look a bit meh and not fresh to me, but the cakes and tarts are worth to try.
Good cafe to hang out after meal.

Btw they don’t list out the price of cakes and pastries.

reviews.author: Liz Han
reviews.text: We had watermelon crushes, decaf iced long blacks, and a decaf latte — all spot on (rare for decaf!). The kids loved the crush. For dessert, we went back to our favourites: blueberry cheesecake, strawberry tart, and the financier. All were just as good, so good they were half gone before I remembered to take photos — the financier is still the winner for me! The vibe was relaxed and quiet enough to enjoy conversations, which made it even better.

properties.name: LAB Rhodes
properties.address: G16/14 Walker St, Rhodes NSW 2138, Australia

reviews.author: Amber PF Mak
reviews.text:
Nice place to chill with good coffee and yummy cakes, buns and pastries!

Had einspanner cold brew coffee and gotta say the cold brew is quite special. Cakes slices are slightly on the higher side, eg a slice of plain cheesecake is $10.20 whereas mostly this is priced around $8-9 in Sydney. Does taste very decent though!

Buns and pastries look amazing, very wide selections. Would love to try the macarons someday, they look very unique! Will be back for more.

reviews.author: munh dawaa
reviews.text: I bought this cake this morning and was shocked to find grey mold inside the cake, including a strawberry that was clearly not fresh. This is a serious hygiene and food safety issue. Mold does not appear overnight, so this cake was clearly not fresh or was stored improperly. Absolutely unacceptable. I will not be returning and do not recommend this place.

reviews.author: soumya sd
reviews.text: Our go to place for coffee and place to meet up. It has good range of snacks and my personal favourite is their tiramisu and all their cakes with coffee. If you are around its definitely worth a stop.

reviews.author: Rebecca Nolan
reviews.text:
Great range of cakes and buns here, both sweet and savoury, I love Asian style desserts this is why this cafe caught my eye.

I tried the pizza hotdog, pretty much exactly what you’d expect, no surprises there. The coffee, unfortunately, was the let-down for me. It tasted quite burnt, and even as someone who usually drinks it black, I ended up adding sugar just to make it drinkable. A simple long black was $5, which feels a bit steep compared to plenty of cafés closer to the city.
That said, I’d come back to sample more of the cakes, because the selection is genuinely good and tempting.

reviews.author: aybota janat
reviews.text:
Today we visited LAB Coffee with our child and ordered three different cakes. However, just like last time, all three cakes were served together on one small plate.

I really didn’t understand why they were combined like that. When three different people order three different cakes, it would be much better, cleaner, and more comfortable if each cake was served on its own plate.

If this is their usual serving style, it may be inconvenient for customers. I hope they can improve this in the future.

Thank you.

reviews.author: Raymond Chan
reviews.text:
Visited LAB in Strathfield and the space feels modern and bright with a lot of seating. The display cabinets are packed with pastries, sliced cakes and mochi. There is a huge range to choose from which makes it fun to browse, almost like walking into a dessert showroom.

I tried the ube sponge cake and the mango mousse cake. The ube cake was soft and light with a mild flavour with a hit of cream. The mango yogurt was refreshing and not too sweet. The fruit was fresh and the yogurt base had a creamy texture without being heavy.

This place is great for choosing pastries to take home or for a late night dessert catch up. There is plenty of seating, the lighting is nice and service at the counter was quick. Prices are on the higher side but the variety and presentation make it worth trying, especially if you like Asian style cakes that are fluffier and less sugary.

Would return to try the bingsu, bread section and more of their mochi.

properties.name: LAB Parramatta
properties.address: Shop 2010A, Level 2, Westfield Parramatta, 159/175 Church St, Parramatta NSW 2150, Australia

reviews.author: Food Mahir & Gardening Ideas
reviews.text: Excellent Coffee and cakes. I got small $5 walnut bite cakes and Papa bun and small tea.m cake. The service is top notch and coffee is perfect. We got long black and latte. They also have a QR code for review and they give free Cream puff for review. The overall ambiance is great. I forgot to take pics initially so apologies for not so good images attached. One small suggestion is to provide some power points under and / or near seating for laptop and / mobile charging. There is one power point though  it away from the seating.

reviews.author: Zong Huan Hoe
reviews.text:
The Cream puff taste so good because the cream is so creamy and  so rich. And the custard bun is yummy so creamy so heavenly. The ice latte is rich in coffee. In total I spent close to $20 Aussie dolllars but I truly in love with it.

The service staff was very nice to me she answered to my question when I ask whether do we still get the free cream puff after we fill the Google review and she even offer help to get the cream puff after I showed her the Google review. Thank you so much for your assistance.

Overall I love the taste of the cream of all the pastry and I also find the ambience of the place quite cosy and not noisy and I can do my work and reading there without any loud disturbance from the surrounding.

reviews.author: sally
reviews.text: Loved the vibe at LAB Parramatta! The food was beautifully presented and tasted amazing! super fresh and full of flavour. The cocktails were creative and perfectly balanced. Service was friendly and quick too. Definitely a great spot for brunch or a night out

properties.name: 4 Stations & Co
properties.address: 4 W Parade, West Ryde NSW 2114, Australia

reviews.author: Sara B
reviews.text: Had a lemon iced tea and tiramisu cup. The best tiramisu I had so far. Iced tea was pretty good too. Loved the service from the female barista, thank you guys!

reviews.author: Fatema Islam
reviews.text: Good coffee. I feel like they need to work on Wagyu burger, they can make it a bit flavourful. My friend ordered pumpkin soup, it felt like the onion over powered. The staff was a bit confused when we asked if the food was halal, and then confirmed with the manager.

reviews.author: Frieren Fern
reviews.text:
I was surprised and couldn’t believe in the way you packed your online order. The cup’s straw opening was left completely unsealed, yet still got placed in the bag without any protection. This caused the coffee to spill over everything. The entire order ended up soaked.

This is the worse packing I’ve ever seen in Sydney. Could you please be more professional and make sure the drinks are properly sealed with your future customers?

properties.name: The Wedge
properties.address: 53/55 Glebe Point Rd, Glebe NSW 2037, Australia
properties.summary: Bright, funky space with corrugated walls and red stools, making sandwiches and specialty coffee.

reviews.author: The Laundry Foundry
reviews.text:
We just had lunch at The Wedge, it's our favourite café in Glebe for a quick bite and coffee. It’s always a great spot to relax and refuel.

The chicken sambo is hands down our favourite thing on the menu! It's consistently fresh, tasty and super satisfying. My son is a big fan of the ham and cheese croissant, which never lasts long once it hits the table.

Great coffee, friendly service and owners and a reliable go-to in the neighbourhood. Highly recommended!

reviews.author: Yunita Fransiska
reviews.text:
As someone who works in hospitality for almost 10 years, just want to give a genuine review to this shop as it was below my expectations.

We got here without knowing that reservation was needed to get seated and the main guy (probably the owner or manager) seated us kindly. We were seated by the window on the ground floor.

We waited for a bit but noone came to tell us its table service, so I went up checking on the baristas if its table service as noone came back to check with us for drinks. I asked the baristas if its table service and they werent even friendly when they answered to me its table service. Head back to the table, waited for the staff to come and take our order.

It took more than 15 minutes for our coffee to came out and more than 20 minutes for the pastries and more than 20 minutes for a smoothie.

Food and drinks service was very slow, coffee was average and no presentation on the drinks. I was going to try the pastries and wait for a bit before order some lunch for us but we were disappointed.

They have good selection of pastries and food, nice seatings and location, friendly waitstaff, but I’m not sure if i’d come back to here.

Maybe it was us and our expectations 😅

reviews.author: Mahsa Naseri
reviews.text:
I recently visited The Wedge in Glebe for breakfast and ordered the Lisbon Breakfast. The café has a pleasant atmosphere. I sat by the window, which was nice for the natural light, although the view wasn’t ideal because a couple of cars were parked right outside. That part isn’t within the café’s control, but it’s worth keeping in mind if you expect a scenic outlook.

The service was great. My order came out quickly, and the staff were warm, attentive, and checked in regularly without being overbearing.

The food was a new experience for me. It included a poached egg served on a special fish, topped with hollandaise sauce on a hash brown. I enjoy trying new dishes, and this one was genuinely nice. I do think the plate would benefit from some vegetables or a small salad on the side, just to balance the meal a bit more.

I also had a matcha latte. I usually only drink matcha at Korean or Japanese cafés, but I decided to try it here. It was surprisingly good and not too sweet, which I prefer.

Overall, it was a very good experience, and I would recommend The Wedge in Glebe for breakfast. In terms of price, it was reasonable for the area, but considering the portion size, it didn’t feel fully worth it.

reviews.author: Sudarat Thamrongthatsanee
reviews.text:
I recently visited Social Butterfly Tea & Dessert and had such a lovely experience. The atmosphere is truly beautiful — calm, peaceful, and thoughtfully designed. It’s the kind of place where you can relax and enjoy your time without feeling rushed.

The tea selection is wonderful, especially the green tea, which was fragrant and perfectly balanced. The cakes were also delicious. Everything felt carefully prepared and presented with attention to detail.

The service was excellent at every level. The staff were warm, attentive, and genuinely welcoming, which made the visit even more enjoyable.

It’s a perfect spot for working quietly, reading a book, or having a relaxed conversation with friends. I would highly recommend this place to anyone looking for a cozy and peaceful tea experience.

reviews.author: Ollie
reviews.text:
Thanks Casper for a fantastic night. We had a long day of events this Saturday and were drawn in by a sign out in front leading to a lift - well, let’s just say that paved a way to something very interesting.

I’m only just getting into tea now after practically being a soft drink and water person, and I was really impressed by the thorough craft of the caterers and the gentle service provided by the staff, it was a wonderful experience and I’d love to come back. In saying that, the dessert and tea quality was brilliant and pleased the taste buds with a balance across bitter and sweet; I really liked it.

The overall atmosphere was lovely and lowkey, it felt like a great place to wind down and I’d definitely entertain the idea of coming here after a long day or a post-work wind down during the week. Great music, good ambiance, quiet chatter. Casper (or Kasper if it’s with a K!) was kind enough to entertain our conversation and tell us his brief story of coming down to Sydney - thanks for sharing and welcome in mate!

Apparently, this place only opened up 2 weeks ago and I strongly believe it’s off to a strong start. It’s a little quiet and quaint, and while I do wish for great success in the future, I’m glad it’s all our little secret for now as it’ll definitely see great patronage ahead. Thanks team for your warm smiles, warm tea and lovely service.

Would I come back? Yep.

reviews.author: JJJie cong_1115
reviews.text:
It was such a cozy atmosphere here, we have never experience a cafe in Sydney like this! This place has its own magic. The owner and the team is very welcoming and they will explain what teas suits you, and their cold brew tea is wonderful! Not too sweet, and their dessert is WONDERFUL! Especially the cloud cake!

Will definitely visit again!

reviews.author: JoeyEats
reviews.text: Stitch broadway was a better experience than QVB. More spacious and relaxed setting. Loved the chicken sando and tomato bagel.

reviews.author: Harry Jackson
reviews.text: First breakfast in Sydney! Cream cheese and tomato bagel - 9/10 Garlic cream cheese bagel -10/10 Mushroom toastie - 8/10. Food was absolutely great the highlight being the garlic cream cheese bagel. Had the Bolivian Moose cascara as well and was blown away by the fruity, tea like, flavour. Very refreshing on a hot day like today 🥵

reviews.author: Max P
reviews.text:
Only been there for coffee in the morning and it was too good. I ordered the seasonal special white rabbit and the strawberry matcha latte which was great!

The white rabbit was a creamy long black with hazelnut flavour and had a dried fruit-like aroma/flavor with slightly sweet finished. It is one of my favorite blend so far. I ended up buying their coffee beans as well

The cafe interior is unique, warm and cozy. Might be a bit crowded in coffee rush hour but pretty sure that everyone can wait for a good coffee. Some staff was friendly but some wasn’t (from what I experienced). However, overall was too good to not coming back.

reviews.author: Danica Coruno
reviews.text: Loved their cakes, tried the Earl grey and sesame cakes, they're both fluffy and velvety. Loved their cream puff as well. The strawberry matcha latte drink tastes great too. It looks like a small Bakery from the outside, but was surprised to know they have bigger space at the back. Busy place but still able to talk. Very nice place.

reviews.author: Jane D
reviews.text:
Friendly customer service. I bought their Mango Green Tea Cake Slice. It was worth the price as the fruit was fresh as a topping and the cake was soft. I really enjoyed the combination of flavours. I would definitely return.
P.S. Card surcharge for purchases under $15

reviews.author: ellen
reviews.text: The staff are very kind. I had a frustrating experience at another shop earlier and was quite flustered, so I didn’t even think about buying candles. One of your staff members kindly asked me and made sure to include candles for me, which I truly appreciated.

properties.name: Ampersand Cafe & Bookstore
properties.summary: Hearty breakfasts and cafe bites in a book-lined venue with a living room vibe and laneway seating.

reviews.author: Von Loo (Vondelicious)
reviews.text: Spent a morning here recently and really loved the atmosphere. It’s set across three floors of books, which gives it a very quiet, cozy vibe—apparently, it was even ranked as one of the most beautiful book-cafés in the world this year. We tried the Turkish eggs and the green bowl; both were fresh and had great flavor, which was a nice surprise as sometimes "themed" cafes can be a bit hit-or-miss with the food. It’s a great little sanctuary in Paddington if you want to eat surrounded by books.

reviews.author: Mojgan Rezaei
reviews.text:
Highly recommended. We had an amazing experience at this café, truly unique and memorable. The staff were wonderful, and the manager, Payam, was exceptionally respectful and professional. When someone mistakenly sat at our reserved table, he handled the situation with outstanding customer service and grace.
The food was absolutely delicious, and we thoroughly enjoyed every bite. When we shared our feedback, we were told the café is ranked number six in the world, which comes as no surprise. Congratulations to the owners, this café truly deserves its place among the best.

reviews.author: Aurélie • Boost Yourself / Mova Mova Coaching
reviews.text:
What can I say about this place, except that I had a truly wonderful time there. The coffee is absolutely divine, and the atmosphere — with books spread across several levels — has everything I love to feel inspired, unwind, and think about the growth of my business.

I highly recommend it; the vibes are excellent. And just for the record, the beef cheek pasta is a must-try — absolutely incredible.

properties.name: La Lune Market
properties.address: 1/13 George St, North Strathfield NSW 2137, Australia

reviews.author: Shellie Wilkes
reviews.text:
You couldn’t ask for much more from a cafe. Don’t be deterred by pre-prepared food, because it was excellent. I had the chicken salad and my partner had one of the toasties and it tasted as though it had been prepared then and there, not to mention when I couldn’t finish it, it was quick and easy to take with me! On top of that, the cold and warm drinks menu was both vast and delicious; rarely can I go to a cafe with my coffee loving partner and get something that I took thing is delicious. My partner is hard to please with coffee and thought their cold brew was excellent, and I was hooked from the first sip of my iced matcha latte.
The place has a great vibe too. Casual without being devoid of character, music without that somehow both doesn’t take over the conversation but it also very cool, and so many seating options to suit whatever mood you’re in (especially offering plenty of outdoor and seating aswell).
The service was friendly without overcompensating and time wasting, drinks and food both came out super fast despite it being busy, and on that, even though it was at full capacity indoors, it didn’t feel suffocatingly busy like some cafes do.
We have a weekly activity here and like to try new cafes every Saturday morning, but I can’t imagine us going anywhere else now.

Oh and last but not least they have a cute little smattering of gift shop types items you can purchase such as coffee beans, card games, all of which are beautifully displayed too.

Give it a try, you won’t regret it.

reviews.author: Music School
reviews.text:
We absolutely love the overall menu at La Lune Café and visit often because it never disappoints.

One thing we especially enjoy is the coffee — it has such a distinct and beautiful flavour. As coffee lovers, we really appreciate how unique and well-crafted it is.

And every time we have the moist, fluffy scones or the bagel with spring-onion cream cheese, it genuinely fills us with happiness. The freshness and quality here are on a different level, and it always leaves us delighted.

Thank you for creating such a wonderful experience every time.

reviews.author: Lysha
reviews.text: This is one of my top cafes in Sydney! I ALWAYS order the mango smoothie (one of the best in Sydney I'd say), and all of the croffles are super tasty. The tiramisu is a standout with the cream and fluffiness of the croffle tasting so good. My favourite part is definitely the atmosphere and seating! Inside feels like you're in South Korea, and is beautifully and comfortably designed!

reviews.author: J Wang
reviews.text: We went there for breakfast. The service was good and generous. The price was reasonable. We ordered big breakfast and the smash avocado, they tasted good, however the sausage tasted weird. I would suggest adding the spinach or avocado on the big breakfast. The cafe has a garden setting with a fish pond, very nice and relaxing.

reviews.author: Sophie R
reviews.text: beautiful laid-back cafe with koi fish pond very easy to read menu nice staff, nicely made cappuccino/ good coffee blend. Pet friendly and lots of seating space. Indoor and outdoor seating. Able to help yourself to table water. Only downside was the price of the meals and what you are getting/quality. Wasn’t bad quality just was expecting more.

reviews.author: Nehmat Kaur
reviews.text: I moved to this neighbourhood a year ago and have often visited for the beautiful space and good food. Unfortunately, the service has consistently fallen short. Today, when my husband noticed the pancakes were different from usual and we politely asked about it, we felt dismissed by a comment suggesting we might not know the difference between pancakes and crêpes. I value service as much as food, and repeated cold experiences have made us feel unwelcome.

reviews.author: Rubes
reviews.text: It’s such a strange yet lovely place. I really like the atmosphere here and the matcha too. The café isn’t crowded, there are just a few seats, and it’s not noisy at all, which makes it perfect for chatting. I said “strange” because it’s both a café and a clothing store, but I actually love that! You can sip your drink while checking out cool fashion pieces at the same time.

reviews.author: Teck Hooi Gan
reviews.text:
Found this cafe somewhere hidden behind all tall buildings in the city.

Tried my luck, ordered a a normal hot long black, cold brew and a special “Moon River” to try.

Good beans, nice environment, very muji.

reviews.author: Jihye Kim
reviews.text: Such a fancy spot! Loved their matcha latte and cold brew. The vibe here is so nice — I’ll definitely be back!

reviews.author: Hoai Anh Nguyen
reviews.text: Best crepes, best strawberry matcha I’ve ever had so far. The crepes are freshly made, so soft and creamy, not too sweet. The drinks are consistently delicious. The shop has a cozy, welcoming vibe. The staffs are lovely ⭐️⭐️⭐️⭐️⭐️

reviews.author: William Yang
reviews.text:
Bubble Nini was an absolute hit at our wedding and Lara was wonderful to work with from start to finish. The bubble tea was fresh, delicious and exactly what everyone needed on a hot day.  'Sun & Moon' was a hit throughout and our guests loved how Bubble Nini was just that extra bit luxurious as a bubble tea.

Bubble Nini added such a fun and refreshing touch to our celebration and we couldn’t recommend them more. We’ve always loved their bubble tea casually and it was just as perfect as part of our wedding celebration as well.

reviews.author: Eddie Leung
reviews.text:
Lovely little cafe with great vibes ☺️

I ordered a matcha drink and it came with a small flower on the table — such a simple but thoughtful touch. I brought this back for my wife and she was genuinely so happy, it really made her day.

Drinks were delicious, presentation was beautiful, and the atmosphere was warm and cosy. You can tell they care about the little details.

Highly recommend if you’re after good drinks and a nice experience 🌼💚

reviews.author: Anthony Carter
reviews.text: The service and atmosphere at Berry Boy Açai was fantastic. We travelled all the way from the south west of Sydney after seeing them all over the socials and it wasn’t a disappointment. I was pleased with the recommendation and glad we drove out to Hurstville to try it. We will definitely be back.

reviews.author: katrina tran
reviews.text:
Absolutely love this place!
The portion sizes are so generous – definitely worth every dollar. The açaí was fresh, texture was on point, and they don’t skimp on the sauce (which I really appreciate!). Great value for money, and I left feeling satisfied from my late night dessert run. Highly recommend!!

reviews.author: Paige
reviews.text: DELICIOUS. The best 😄 the facility needs air conditioning for hot days. But the açai is on point.

properties.name: 1606 Luis de Torres
properties.address: Shop 7/7 Rider Blvd, Rhodes NSW 2138, Australia

reviews.author: Yinnie Zhou
reviews.text: Obsessed with this café’s vibe—soft lighting, cozy seating, and such a calm, welcoming atmosphere! Their latte is creamy with a rich coffee flavor that hits just right. Service is friendly, and it’s the perfect spot to unwind with a drink. Highly recommend for coffee lovers!

reviews.author: Roxy
reviews.text: An absolutely fantastic brunch spot! I’m especially in love with their Iced Earl Grey Latte — it’s incredibly smooth and fragrant, with a perfectly balanced tea aroma that’s simply addictive. The brunch menu offers a great variety of both Chinese and Western dishes, so there’s something for everyone! My favorite is the Chinese Breakfast Set B: youtiao (fried dough), tea egg, and doufunao (tofu pudding). It tastes just like home! For brunch, I always pick their Chicken Mushroom Risotto. Whenever I can’t decide where to go for lunch, coming here is always the right choice. Cozy atmosphere and friendly service — definitely my go-to weekend spot!

reviews.author: Jarrod McCloughan
reviews.text:
My weekend morning haunt after gym.
The best Ham and Cheese croissant in town! Absolutely love them! Beautiful Taro Lattes as well.
Staff are always friendly and always remember me even though I only go once a week!

reviews.author: Jessica Nguyen
reviews.text:
I tried Bingsu at this dessert store and based on my experience, I will vote 3 stars as nothing special. I tried 2 favours (strawberry and matcha), but the taste is really the same, just colour different. The fruit topping was not fresh as well.
Won't come back for Bingsu.

reviews.author: Ash L
reviews.text: I came here around in January and ordered a Korean egg drop from the menu. Unfortunately, it seems there has been changes of the chief and he didn’t know how to made it and served something like a burger / beckon and egg roll. I explained to the front staff for what is egg drop sandwich and how the egg should be mixed. They said they got it and  and did it a second time however nothing seemed changed except for they added a different sauce. They should remove things from the menu or let customers know if they are not available or don’t know how to make it.

reviews.author: EMILY LU
reviews.text:
We ordered green tea bingsu.

Very bland flavour. They just dust green tea powder on top of vanilla ice cream which other shops they serve the actual green tea ice cream. The bingsu is vanilla flavour as well. Not green tea. Quite disappointed.

Cheese cake and chocolate cake are very dense.

reviews.author: Hannah Chan
reviews.text: I really enjoyed the big breakfast at Sunbrew Cafe. The food was fresh, well-cooked, and very filling. Great service and a nice atmosphere too. Definitely worth a visit!

reviews.author: S A (Ms QUASON)
reviews.text: What a gem!!! Tucked away on a side street in Narwee, this cafe is a true gem. The coffee is excellent, the service warm and welcoming, and the food absolutely outstanding. The crispy chills eggs is a must-try—perfectly executed with delicious bread. We’ll definitely return to explore more of the menu. With its delicious food, friendly staff, and reasonable prices, this is hands down the best local cafe in the area. They also serve pepe saya butter with their toast and showcase Sonoma baked goods, bringing a touch of the East and Inner West’s quality without the need for travel. Highly recommend this spot for both coffee and meals! I just wish if they have halal meat or other pork offering on the breakfast menu.

reviews.author: Tim Shu
reviews.text: A lovely cafe located down a side street in Narwee. The coffee is great, the service is friendly, and the food is expectational. Highly recommend the eggs bene, so good and the bread is delicious. We'll definitely be back to try other dishes on the menu.

properties.name: Harry's House Cafe
properties.address: 248 Canley Vale Rd, Canley Heights NSW 2166, Australia

reviews.author: Nora Lee
reviews.text: We ordered gelato, chocolate bingsu, the flan and the coconut matcha. The flan was big enough to serve 2/3 and came with toppings on the side (boba balls and shaved ice). It was delicious and not too sweet. The bingsu serving size fed 5 of us and had so many different, delicious toppings. Highly recommend! The matcha wasn’t such a hit though since it was quite bitter. The vibe was nice, a small cafe with soft music and dimmed lights. Overall an 8/10 experience

reviews.author: Tina Le
reviews.text:
Terrible customer experience. I had contacted the store after leaving behind a bag of food and had just wanted to know if it was still there or not so I could return and pick it up.

The male employee who picked up was extremely rude and stated “no” without checking and hung up. Contacted them once more and didn’t get to speak much before they hung up again.

After contacting the store for a third time, the male employee stated that he was “too busy” check and didn’t want to take a look outside. The store was mostly empty when we left so I was confused as to why they couldn’t quickly check and provide a proper answer.

Whether the food was still there or not, I wouldn’t even be sure since I wasn’t given a proper answer nor any assurance.

The Fruit chunks with durian was also made with fruit that wasn’t fresh so it left a weird after taste. Overall, the terrible customer service will be a reason not to return.

reviews.author: Kathleen Young
reviews.text: Very interesting and nice desserts with a Vietnamese and Korean flavour. The iced desserts are delicious, my favourite being the matcha flavour. The Vietnamese egg coffee was very cute, with an interesting flavour and very strong coffee! The complementary cold tea was also very refreshing. Wish they would be more environmentally friendly and stop using plastic spoons especially when dining in.

properties.name: Sugarbaby Espresso
properties.address: shop7/256 Coward St, Mascot NSW 2020, Australia

reviews.author: Kithmini Cooray
reviews.text:
10/10 would recommend. Such lovely service. And the shakshuka eggs were amazing! The only tip is to reduce the sugar slightly.
And the French toast was perfect 👌 such a good balance of soft inside and crispy on the edges. Thank you for a lovely brunch.

reviews.author: P L
reviews.text: It's a small cafe but the coffee was really good. I like the little touches such as the quaint crooked teaspoon!

reviews.author: MJ Xuan-An Le
reviews.text: It is a cute cafe. Located close to mascot station. Ordered a coffee and eggs benedict - at the time it was fine; good, delicious. However went got home, got really sick (it was the pretty much the only thing I ate from that point on for the rest of the day and remained unwell, bathroom wasn’t fun) after eating it… hopefully it was just a one off thing but it certainly felt like food poisoning or similar at the time, that’s unfortunately why a star or two is lost -otherwise service was quick and staff friendly.

reviews.author: Andre Park
reviews.text: We had lunch here a couple of weeks ago. We ordered the creamy pasta and a waffle. The pasta was delicious and the staff were friendly. The downside was that the food took a little while to arrive, and the chocolate on the waffle wasn't the greatest quality, but everything else was good or average.

reviews.author: Awatif
reviews.text: The café is really lovely and the coffee is absolutely fantastic. I loved how they offer a wide variety of desserts at different price points, and they also serve breakfast. Unfortunately, the kitchen ventilation wasn’t working during my visit, so the entire place was filled with cooking smells. It was quite unpleasant and took away from the otherwise beautiful atmosphere. I hope they fix this issue because the café truly deserves more visits.

reviews.author: catherine j
reviews.text:
Lovely customer service, and friendly people!
Food was great (chicken schnitzel roll), and the coffees are perfect (Iced Latte and large soy cap)

reviews.author: Annie Le
reviews.text:
My favourite local coffee shop & the perfect post-run stop this Xmas morning 🎄🏃‍♀️
I had an iced pure matcha & the tender chicken breast salad bowl. Both were spot on. The matcha was smooth & refreshing. The salad was fresh, light & satisfying, & the chicken breast was beautifully soft & tender (just like the name 😉)
Great coffee, great food, & a lovely local vibe. Always a pleasure coming here - highly recommend!

reviews.author: ChillGuy
reviews.text:
Good atmosphere for a cafe that’s situated on the main road of Kingsgrove. The interiors for the cafe is quite well maintained and nice.

Unfortunately though there’s about where the good things end.

I ordered the chicken salad, which came with veggies that weren’t fresh. Browning of the greens was pretty off setting.

Chicken was cooked to perfection I’ll give them that. But there was no real seasoning. And the photos on the sign board were a bit misleading as it showed two poached eggs which didn’t come with me. (I guess you’d have to order to the add-ons for that)

The cafe is surprisingly busy as well. But for my own preference the coffee tasted a bit weak for me. You may like it if you prefer a slightly lighter roast.

Should also mention that tables were seated on was also dirty and unclean. Had to request for staff to give it a wipe down. (Not the end of the world)

Overall, I’m giving the place the benefit of the doubt. Maybe the main head chef was on holiday leave and I simply got unlucky? 🤷‍♂️

Personally though I expected more.

reviews.author: Klaudia W.O
reviews.text: We always go to Ickle at least once a week. Honestly, the coffee here is one of the best in Sydney! The team there are always so nice and friendly. It is a dog friendly cafe, and we love that! They serve great food too. Our favourite ones are their avocado toast, congee and kimchi toast :) highly recommended this place.

properties.name: Eleven Miles
properties.address: 1/324 Crown St, Surry Hills NSW 2010, Australia

reviews.author: Goong Ging
reviews.text: Perfect coffee and food. Staff are lovely and very friendly. I ordered the bagel . it was very good. The bagel was crispy and very tasty. I highly recommend all the bagels and coffee. They are really good.

reviews.author: Ashlyn K
reviews.text: The food and coffee here are so good! I’ve come back several times . Especially for the oat mocha, I’m obsessed with it!.

reviews.author: Seus Ku
reviews.text: Haven’t been to a cafe for a long time and always want to check this place out. Very cute atmosphere, lots of plants. We sat in the garden at the back. So relaxing. We ordered chicken caesar salad with spicy dressing and ice latte. We finished with salted caramel muffin and lemon muffin. Delicious! Perfect late lunch for two. Amy, the owner is super friendly.

properties.name: Redroof Cafe
properties.address: Corner of Homebush Road &, Fairholm St, Strathfield NSW 2135, Australia

reviews.author: KC O
reviews.text: Fantastic little cozy cafe by the park. Serving very delicious cafe food. The tater tots and milk shakes are amongst the must orders. Service was speedy and staff were very friendly and helpful!

reviews.author: April Liu
reviews.text: Had a mix-up with my order at Redroof Cafe — it wasn’t sent to the kitchen, so we waited over 30 minutes. But the staff and owner handled it well then, with sincere apologies and free snacks, coffees to make up for it. The food was absolutely delicious, and their attitude was warm and genuine. Great service recovery and truly worth a 5-star review!

reviews.author: Lil Aussie Traveller
reviews.text:
- Friendly customer service, staff are always polite and accommodating. Service can be a little slow sometimes.
- Dog friendly
- Nice decor with the staghorn ferns & cute budgerigars
- Cool shelter for hot days
- Coffee is good
- Prices are reasonable (for Sydney), when I was last there they did a toastie and coffee deal for about $12
- Toilet at the back (you need to turn on the tap below the basin to allow water flow).

properties.name: Silk Cafe & Restaurant
properties.address: World Square, Shop 18/123 Liverpool St, Sydney NSW 2000, Australia

reviews.author: Tadd Lyons
reviews.text:
[  ] Locating/Ease of Access: 10/10
Located in the heart of World Square, under the escalators leading up to the bar, this Cafe was a place we have passed a few times, but finally stopped in to eat. It was breakfast during the week.

[  ] Service/Wait Times: 10/10
The service was really quick, polite and friendly. We were served by a few people, all of which were accomodating.

[  ] Atmosphere/Feel: 9/10
The Cafe was tastefully decorated with artworks, all of which were for sale - it definitely suited the space. The layout definitely worked well.

[  ] Food/Drink: 10/10
The menu looked outstanding, and I ordered the food expecting to be underwhelmed. I was shocked when the food looked exactly like the menu, and tasted just as good. Very impressed!

[  ] Other: 10/10
49/50 - Overall the experience was incredibly pleasant. I would highly recommend stopping by if in World Square.

reviews.author: 하나서
reviews.text: Friendly staff and quick service! It's a great cafe for family meals. It's fun to taste various menus. We're looking forward to this store. I recommend it!

reviews.author: T T
reviews.text: It’s a lovely place to have dinner in the middle of the city. The staff were so wonderful and kind. The food was delicious and wanting to come back to try more of their dishes. Absolutely pleasure dining here.

properties.name: Malika Bakehouse
properties.address: 515 Crown St, Surry Hills NSW 2010, Australia

reviews.author: Cassandra Leung
reviews.text:
Malika Bakehouse is officially my new post-gym obsession. Everything was fresh, flavourful and beautifully made.

We had the sucuk & egg on a simit bagel (so good and perfectly balanced), the spinach, feta & ricotta börek which was flaky and comforting, and the baklava waffles… unreal. The Biscoff cookie and Turkish delights were the perfect sweet finish.

Highly recommend the iced pistachio coconut cloud matcha — creamy, nutty and not overly sweet.

Such a great mix of savoury and sweet options, generous portions and a welcoming vibe. Will definitely be back.

reviews.author: The Third Bite
reviews.text: I had the steak sandwich with fries and it was absolutely delicious. The gozleme was fresh, light, and full of flavour, genuinely divine. You order at the counter and the service is friendly, quick, and on point. The food came out fast and everything tasted excellent. I wanted to try more but this was plenty and I probably didn’t need the chips. Malika Bakehouse was terrific, the space is airconditioned with comfortable seating and a really nice atmosphere. The coffee was amazing and hit the spot perfectly. I forgot to take a pic of the sandwich, but stuff it, the taste made up for it.

reviews.author: Emily Li
reviews.text: A beautiful little cafe in Surry Hills that’s super close to the light rail! The food was great and the drinks were also amazing! A big thank you to the staff Lee and Ayda, and to the kitchen staff Guzde and Tak for helping me and my guest feel welcome. I’d love to visit again whenever I’m in the area!

properties.name: The Brothers Project Cafe
properties.address: Shop 2/755-759 Botany Rd, Rosebery NSW 2018, Australia

reviews.author: Jiantao Wei
reviews.text: There are many hidden gem restaurants in Rosebery, but I was still blown away by the brunch at this place. The shop is spacious and bright, making it very comfortable to dine in. The service was excellent, and all the dishes recommended to us were delicious. Both the coffee and matcha were smooth and enjoyable. The ingredients are fresh, creatively combined, and taste absolutely fantastic. My favourite was their "Brûlée French Toast"—every bite was pure enjoyment. Highly recommend this brunch spot.

reviews.author: Z Z
reviews.text:
Nice, clean and spacious cafe, good for dating and gathering.

Cold brew is on the traditional high caffeine and dark roast style, not much acidity but more on the bitter and brown sugar roasting profile side.

The food is rather small proportioned but tastes good. Tom yum pasta will fit your assumption how it should be. If there is a slice of lime on the plate for the extra acidity can be even better, but only personal preference. Love the fluffy toast, guilty pleasure with the cheese and cream.

reviews.author: Pallavi
reviews.text:
Had a lovely weekday brunch here. I had heard about, and was advised to try their French Toasts.
I tried the Brûlée French toast, and it was absolutely delicious. Melt in the mouth, soft and fresh. We had a fried chicken sandwich too which was tasty. Coffee is great too. They only have eggs for breakfast till 11am though, so consider going early if you like eggs for breakfast.
They have ample indoor and outdoor space to sit and tables are far apart enough to have a conversation. Service is good too.

reviews.author: Z Z
reviews.text:
Nice and cozy cafe next to Green Square station. Will have some bonus if you are Popmart (or Labubu) lover.

Cold brew is made from espresso blend, got punchy, strong taste and very caffeinated (when you need it).  Size is generous for dine in, and it comes with a nice garnish. Matcha latte is defaulted with no sugar, which is kind of nice to enjoy the ‘all matcha’ flavour.

Chicken sandwich is delicious - bread is toasted just right with golden brown surface, cheese is perfectly just melted, chicken is tender. The flavour of ‘New Orlean’ marinate can be slightly stronger but in general I liked it quite a lot.

reviews.author: F A
reviews.text:
Finding a spot in Zetland that actually feels like a "local" can be tough with all the new apartments, but this little gem nailed it.

Honestly, the coffee here is on another level. You can tell they take their beans seriously, I ended up buying a bag to take home because the house roast was that good.

Perfectly balanced and zero bitterness.
What really makes the place, though, is the vibe. The owners and the staff are super good people; they’re actually friendly and remember your order instead of just rushing you through.

It’s rare to find a shop where the team genuinely seems happy to be there.
If you’re around Green Square or living in the 2017 area, definitely drop in.

Great drinks, even better people. 10/10.

reviews.author: Nicholas Lelos
reviews.text: Pleasant and good location corner cafe with good selection of drinks. It has changed ownership a few times, with a change of style. At the moment it has interesting choices of drinks, and about 8 hit food items with a great selection of pastries. Prices are a bit steep but nothing unusual for the area. Good location and close to Green Square. Problematic space inside it is a bit cramped

properties.name: Sorry Thanks I Love You
properties.address: 188 Pitt Street Level One Westfield, Sydney NSW 2000, Australia

reviews.author: Sarah charles (Thewhereto)
reviews.text: We were in the city recently and found this cute little concept store and cafe ☕️ 😊👌@sorrythanksiloveyou ♥️in the middle of @westfieldsyd and they had great coffee, pastries and a lovely place to relax in quiet. Great to pop in if you find yourself in the city

reviews.author: CEETIZEN
reviews.text: After a long morning doing window shopping I came across the coffee shop  I chose this one instead of the other two in the upper levels because they are too small and crowded. Sorry, thanks, I love you is on the lower ground floor which gives that underground vibe plus has good space to sit.

reviews.author: Diana Waines
reviews.text: Cool modern quirky coffee shop with charming staff polite & super friendly.  Coffee was also a big hit for me. Very smooth with flavour flavour flavour! Water was an added nice touch in a super cute bottle & glass

properties.name: Dulcet Cafe (in Kinokuniya)
properties.address: Located in Books Kinokuniya, Level 2/500 George St, Sydney NSW 2000, Australia

reviews.author: Sophie Nguyen
reviews.text: Dulcet Cafe is such a lovely spot! Their cakes are absolutely amazing — beautiful, delicious, and made with so much care. Every bite is full of flavor, and they look as good as they taste. The café itself has a cozy, welcoming vibe, perfect for a relaxed coffee or afternoon treat. The staff are friendly and attentive, which makes the experience even better. Definitely a must-visit if you love great coffee and incredible cakes!

reviews.author: Sherry Z
reviews.text:
A party without a cake is called a MEETING! I brought this Christmas limited cake to a weekend staycation with friends and they were all impressed by this cake! The tastes and the shape are both stunning.
Actually this is my 5th year buying cakes, bakery and dessert from Dulcet. Glad to see they keep launching new products!

reviews.author: TINAEATEAT
reviews.text: Their cakes are so good😋 I come here almost once a week!! The new Christmas cakes are great too!! I got the matcha one and it’s perfect for sharing with family ♥️

properties.name: Gram Cafe & Pancakes
properties.address: Chatswood Interchange, Shop 67, Level 3/436 Victoria Ave, Chatswood NSW 2067, Australia

reviews.author: Isabel
reviews.text:
Decent cafe for pancakes and brunch 🍓

Despite having pancakes in their name, I enjoyed their french toast more than the soufflé pancakes. Tried their savoury and sweet options and the food were quite yummy but I feel it’s quite expensive. The matcha french toast was made BEAUTIFULLY which paired well with the ice cream and strawberries. The mochi soufflé pancake tasted a bit dry and bland, same with the karaage pancakes. The chicken burger was very dense and was a big portion too.
Service was good and the atmosphere was clean and easy to talk in.

reviews.author: Trista Li
reviews.text: I have been here before when they were still somewhat new and a few times after covid.  The pancakes were not like before.  It has gone downhill since after covid but I thought maybe they were busy.  When I went today, it wasn't busy.  They tasted the same so flavour was still good.  However, they were not as fluffy anymore which was disappointing as they were supposed to be souffle pancakes which were supposed to be fluffy.  They were definitely fluffier when I first went.

reviews.author: Kylie Diep
reviews.text:
We did not have to wait to get in and the store is decorated very cute. The portion is actually huge for a dessert so we had to share between 2 people.

We ordered the premium pancakes with additional vanilla ice cream and hojicha ice cream. The pancakes are super fluffy and delicious. The vanilla ice cream tastes just like full cream milk, I couldn’t taste any hint of vanilla though. The hojicha is very special and different with bittersweet taste of tea.

Definitely come back again to try different dishes.

properties.name: Dirty Red Glebe
properties.address: 41 Glebe Point Rd, Glebe NSW 2037, Australia

reviews.author: edelicious blog
reviews.text: A spacious spot with both indoor and outdoor seating. Friendly service and food came out in a timely manner. I ordered the fish tacos — crispy battered flathead tucked into soft shells, loaded with fresh lettuce, smashed avo, a kick of Tabasco, and creamy sour cream. They were okay! The coffee, though, was really good.

reviews.author: Alice Ab
reviews.text:
The food itself was good overall and nicely presented — no complaints there. However, a few things took away from the experience.

The scrambled eggs were completely unseasoned, which was surprising given how simple that is to get right. A little salt and pepper would’ve gone a long way.

It was also a very hot day and the table water was warm — at the very least, some ice would’ve been appreciated.

Lastly, there was a sneaky 10% Saturday surcharge added to the bill. This wasn’t clearly communicated and is something I haven’t encountered before, so it felt a bit off-putting.

The total bill came to $77 for two meals, one coffee, and one juice, which felt a little steep considering the above issues.

There’s definitely potential here, but attention to these small details would make a big difference to the overall experience.

Will I return? Probably not.

reviews.author: rakel
reviews.text: love the interior of dirty red, they have plenty of outdoor seating and it just feels so welcoming inside. i sat at their bench, its a perfect spot to bring a journal or do some work on your laptop, and tried their jalapeño cheddar cornbread dish. it was amazing! naturally gluten free, packed with flavour and a wonderful chipotle sauce and i relished every bite of it. officially one of my fav cafes in sydney, i need to grab a coffee or a pastry next time.

reviews.author: Kai S
reviews.text:
Lunch for 3 | $48
Homemade simple comfort food, lovely service and atmosphere

We ordered the most popular menu items as recommended: aunty’s wonton soup, beef pho and steak sandwich.

Wonton soup was yummy, but not Hong Kong authentic level, mostly because noodles weren’t ‘al denté’ like they should be. Soup is more southeastern Asian style than special wonton soup. Wonton filling was really nice - prawn meat was fresh.

Beef pho was pretty good. Authentic good stock, but wish there were more toppings for the price.

Steak sandwich used scotch fillet, was fine too, but very cheap sliced toast. Portion was on the small side.

Overall a pleasant lunch, but there’s room for improvement. (Next time I would choose the dry noodles with wonton etc.)

reviews.author: Z Z
reviews.text:
Another gem on Botany Rd.

The room is spacious, chill and cool with the some art framed on the wall.

Auntie is very friendly and go through the menu with us. The menu consists of classic brunch like sandwich and toast and also Vietnamese egg (dry/soup) noodle with different toppings (pork, chicken, wonton and so on).

It’s nice bowls of noodles - the portion is not huge but it’s reasonable for the pricing. Won ton feels like house made - the filling doesn’t feel like high fat content compared to frozen packaged ones and each one is juicy and comes with one shrimp inside.

Sauce for dry noodle is nice and it’s the kind of sweet savoury you can expect from a good Viet restaurant.

The winner is the broth. Auntie told us there is no msg and is from chicken/pork one. The broth comes with dry noodle tastes clean and full of natural sweetness from pork bone and vegetable.

Will want to go back to try pho and other breakfast items.

reviews.author: Dhammaporn Asawaprecha
reviews.text: We came across this cafe by chance after we went for an exercise class nearby. The owner is very friendly. The food is amazing! It looks good and tasted great. Totally recommend the wonton noodle and the pork dry noodle. The cafe is spacious and well decorated.

reviews.author: Hermine Cholous
reviews.text:
The best (and fastest served) cappuccino in Redfern, we kept coming back everyday!
The food was also very tasty and fresh, and the workers were all very nice as well. Only good things to say! :)

Also they have a fast and free WiFi!!

reviews.author: Carlo Croce
reviews.text:
Redfern is plenty of nice cafes, but blue brew is hands down my favorite.

I missed from Sydney 3 months and I couldn’t wait to come back to have my fix. Both drinks and food are top tier and the staff is always extra nice - they never flinch at my crazy cappucino requests (extra choc, half decaf, soy 😂 - I’m so sorry!).

Thank you for always welcoming me and offering both amazing service and great coffee.

My favorites from the menu are the cold brew and the croissant eggs benedict. But any coffee tastes great. Really one level above any other cafe in the area.

Absolutely recommended!

reviews.author: Phoebe
reviews.text: Decent prices ~$20 average each dish, usual cafe fare dishes, executed very well, very smooth coffee, nice vibes inside and outdoor alfresco dining.very nice spot for catchups w/ friends. Bonus: no Sunday surcharge! (Unlike many other places).

reviews.author: Raymond Chan
reviews.text:
Chill spot for matcha desserts and a relaxed sit down.

Ordered the Biscoff Matcha and it was enjoyable overall. The matcha flavour is present without being too bitter, and it pairs nicely with the sweetness from the Biscoff layers. The texture is soft and creamy, with a good balance between the matcha and cookie notes, though it does lean on the sweeter side.

The space itself is very minimal and clean, lots of light wood, simple seating, and a calm atmosphere. It’s not crowded or noisy, making it a nice place to sit and chat rather than a quick grab and go. Feels more like a quiet dessert café where you can take your time.

reviews.author: Meg.
reviews.text:
Tried this place for the first time today. We’ve wanted to come before but it was always busy with long lines, so we finally got the chance to try it.

The drinks were really good 💖 foamy, creamy, and not too sweet, which made them easy to enjoy. If you’re looking for good drink options, this place is a nice choice. The matcha was good.

The place is nice and cozy, and I love the interior and their bear logo too 🐻✨ cute!

I personally wasn’t a fan of the miso mushroom shokupan (just my preference), but my friend really liked it!

reviews.author: Isabel
reviews.text:
Came to try their matcha, we got the ichigo matcha (strawberry matcha) and matcha coconut. Both were pretty nice but felt the matcha coconut one was a bit on the bitter side. Ichigo matcha was a bit sweeter but the matcha taste could’ve been a bit stronger. We did enjoy it regardless and would definitely come back to try their other drinks and food!
The cafe itself is quite clean and aesthetic, service was friendly too.

properties.name: Sappho Bookstore
properties.address: 51 Glebe Point Rd, Glebe NSW 2037, Australia
properties.summary: Bookstore with a courtyard cafe, offering brekkie and lunch, plus wine, cocktails and share plates.

reviews.author: Tamsin Hughes
reviews.text: Groovy bookshop with heaps of interesting books - but a bit pricey. Charlie, the bookshop cat is adorable and has his own postcards and bookmarks for sale. Nice looking courtyard cafe too.

reviews.author: Nicole S
reviews.text: This is one of my favourite places for coffee and snacks, or dinner and cocktails. Or even buying books! Love it all.

reviews.author: Emilia de Jounge
reviews.text: Bought a book, had one of the bowls, and had a great time sitting and doing work. Great spot to come alone or with someone else. Fantastic atmosphere and vibes. Highly recommend!

properties.name: Smelly Cheesecake
properties.summary: Down-to-earth outpost offering eggs, toast & other brunch dishes, plus coffee & desserts.

reviews.author: zzz
reviews.text:
I had an onion bagel with scallion cream cheese and it was so good. The cream cheese that came with the bagel was generous and I wanted to make the most of it. Hence, I got another plain bagel to smother it with the remaining cream cheese.

My friends each got bacon and egg bagel sandwich and lox bagel sandwich and they were delicious. They serve good coffee as well. I had flat white and it didn’t disappoint.

I enjoyed my dining experience here and would love to come back again to try more items on their menu. Their gelatos look interesting with a wide variety of flavors to choose from. Would love to try them as well.

reviews.author: Geobin G
reviews.text: A very cute cafe tucked away close to the Strathfield railway station. Since this was my first visit, i tried their recommended original cheesecake and it was worth it. They also have a nice illustration about how the cheesecake came to be. Very nice.

reviews.author: Claude B
reviews.text:
Just across Strathfield train station and right in front of the Uber waiting area is this inviting spot, The Smelly Cheesecake.

I tried their Mango Cheesecake, which combines mango, mascarpone, and New York cheesecake. It had the perfect balance of sweetness and creaminess: absolutely 5/5.

I also had their Ice Mango Green Tea Latte. At first, the green tea was quite strong, but after blending it all together, the mango and tea complemented each other beautifully. Another 5/5!

Great food, nice ambiance, and overall a really enjoyable experience. Will definitely come back.

properties.name: Dymocks Cafe
properties.address: Mezzanine Level, 428 George St, Sydney NSW 2000, Australia
properties.summary: Mezzanine venue with booth seating overlooking a bookshop, for all-day breakfasts, cakes and crêpes.

reviews.author: Damian Ennis
reviews.text:
I’ve been a repeat customer, and it never fails to impress. 💚

The food is consistently excellent, the menu detailed, well thought out and extensive.
No matter what you order, the quality is there. Breakfast, lunch, dessert or just tea (not a coffee drinker) and cake - it always delivers (I don’t even have much of a sweet tooth)!

The location and setting is a real highlight - particularly if you are a people-watcher.
Dining on the mezzanine, surrounded by a great bookstore, gives a great atmosphere - a true booklover’s paradise.
Waiting for a table is usually quite quick, though on holidays it can be busy.
Portions are generous and good value for money; even simple dishes like an omelette, a chicken toastie or raisin toast far exceed expectations.

The service is speedy and friendly - a highlight.

I have tried a range of dishes over time - and everything has been delicious.
Tip: the first couple of booths near the entrance are quite small, so it’s worth asking for another seat if possible.

All up, this is a brilliant, slightly hidden gem in the city with a great vibe, excellent food, generous servings and a unique setting.

I’ll be back, again and again. 👍

reviews.author: Scarlett Rose
reviews.text:
Love the atmosphere here! Pity that we can’t study or work here… the Vienna (black) coffee had chocolatey notes, the cream was nice and cool and sprinkled with a tad bit of chocolate.

For the main meal I ordered chicken and veggie soup (comes with sourdough). It has potato’s, chicken (obviously), carrot and other veggies in it. It was warm and the veggies melt into your mouth. Though there were way too many green toppings- it all i could taste in every bite 🙂‍↕️…

The waitress was so lovely and accomodating. She had a kind smile and was polite and patient with me, suggesting options near to what I was trying to explain.

reviews.author: Maria Medvedeva
reviews.text: We arrived absolutely starving! The service was incredibly fast—honestly the quickest we’ve seen in Australia. The food was delicious, and our waitress was so lovely. Thank you for hospitality! ✨

properties.name: XS Espresso Gregory Hills
properties.address: 4/18 Holborn Cct, Gledswood Hills NSW 2557, Australia

reviews.author: Melissa Fabs
reviews.text: This place  doesn’t deserve a star , I went with my daughter yesterday at around 2pm to XS Gregory hills and ordered a big breakfast we sat inside at a corner table with a high bench next to it , we received our food and enjoyed it having a conversation and there was nothing wrong with the food until I was about to finish my food we saw a very big cockroach crawl from under the table we were eating at, we both looked at it in shock it crawled quickly to the top bench near our table that’s when I geabbed the table number and used it to kill the cockroach. We were both in shock and disgusted I felt like I just ate a whole plate of cockroaches, I felt like throwing up. I called the manager who was leaving the restaurant and told her, she said they just had the place sprayed the day before, which means they know they have cockroaches in the premises. I asked for a refund which she did she also offered a voucher to the value of the big breakfast which was $33. I gave my details as she asked for it, about 30 mins after I left the restaurant, I received a call from the owner I thought he was calling to apologise but he rang to attack me and criticise me and asked me questions because he was more concerned about how his staff were feeling! because I received a refund and a voucher. I explained that i don’t want the voucher but I did take the refund he then started acussing me and was very very rude. He wasn’t apologetic and was arrogant  and very rude. It’s not my first time there all the times I’ve been there the staff are friendly but this time I was actually eating with cockroaches. I explained how disgusting I felt and very unhygienic and dirty and then to be treated so rudely by the owner, I’m feeling traumatised, attacked for complaining and very upset by this whole situation.  I will never ever go there again and I don’t want to use the voucher they’ll probably put cockroaches or other insects in my food. Very unhappy and upset customer. Pics of the cockroaches attached..

reviews.author: Carlo Miranda
reviews.text: By far the juiciest, most delicious burgers I've had yet. Also the fries are seasoned well but not overwhelmingly flavoured. My wife and a friend of mine enjoyed our stay too. It's relatively quiet for a roadside Cafe. A very peaceful vibe to the place. Good for a heart to heart.

reviews.author: fadi kareem
reviews.text:
My wife just ordered and have a look to what I received..
At least put something to cover the coffee..
I can’t understand how you give to uber driver like this without the coffee cup cover…
Bad customer service

reviews.author: Nuno Correia
reviews.text: Vivace Café offers a decent option if you’re after a quick gelato fix without breaking the bank. The gelato itself is good enough. It's not the best you’ll ever have, but enjoyable and satisfying for the price. The big plus is that it’s reasonably priced, so it’s a solid choice when you want something sweet and simple without spending too much.

reviews.author: Milz_Cloud
reviews.text:
I have been coming here for ages, recently the acai has been filled extremely low (1/3 of my cup was filled with just strawberries and air)
Lovely service and staff tho!

reviews.author: Alexia Ng
reviews.text: Tiny portions and uneven granola-to-acai proportion don't buy their acai!!! This was $11

properties.name: cafe ACRO+ (in Central Park Mall)
properties.address: Ground Level, 28 Broadway, Chippendale NSW 2008, Australia

reviews.author: Christitina Cc
reviews.text: Absolutely loved the experience here! The coffee was fantastic—smooth, rich, and perfectly brewed. What truly stood out was the exceptional customer service. The staff were warm, attentive, and went above and beyond to make sure everything was just right. Whether you’re a coffee enthusiast or just looking for a cozy spot, this cafe is a must-visit. I will definitely be back!

reviews.author: Camila López
reviews.text: Matcha tasted very light and had lot of crumbs at the bottom. They gave me my matcha as soon as I ordered I was impressed by how fast they made it, however, it seems they didn’t whisked the matcha powder enough. Good service, nice cute little shop.

reviews.author: Nin Roberts
reviews.text: Came on the recommendation of a friend. Not usually a huge fan of bagels as they can often be dry, heavy and hard to chew but this was the opposite! Crunchy on the outside and super soft on the inside - it was toasted just perfect. They also spread some soft of jam instead so even the basic “egg & avo” tasted delicious! Definitely coming back to try the other bagels.

reviews.author: shu lok
reviews.text: Great coffee and a cozy atmosphere. The flat white was smooth and well-made, and the staff were friendly and efficient. A perfect spot to relax.

reviews.author: SydneyGR
reviews.text: They only have drinks here but they made this literally Old TAB into something a bit homely and looking different from other places! You feel like your in someone’s home here. It’s got sum interesting memorabilia too,worth having a look if you want to see something unique

reviews.author: Anne Miller
reviews.text: There are always many people enjoying the area which is shady and offers both indoors and outdoor service. They also take your order to you with a friendly smile.

properties.name: Incafe
properties.address: Shop 1/11/23 Rawson Pl, Haymarket NSW 2000, Australia

reviews.author: A Graham
reviews.text: Chill cafe next to central station and Haymarket light-rail/bus stop. All tables have QR codes to place an order to that table. The menu is super customizable down to the number of sugars and the temperature of the drink. We stayed here for an afternoon after checking out of our hotel before leaving for the airport and it was a very relaxing atmosphere with no problems. There are places to charge electronics, bar seats, tables, and couches. We ordered lunch here and the burger was pretty good too. Lunch plates come with fries, salad, and a drink. Would recommend!

reviews.author: Stephanie
reviews.text: Had a dirty chai and latte which was delicious and we were promptly served with water when we arrived however the noise around the area was horrible. There were loud sirens going off for the span of our visit. Not very busy but was very loud with chatter, maybe the sound moved more strongly here. Lots of seating available. Wifi available.

reviews.author: Amelie
reviews.text: Calm atmosphere with a mixture of tables and sofas to sit on. Lovely pastries, especially the almond croissant. The coffee was good - we noticed it was significantly better quality when we had it to have in house rather than takeaway.

reviews.author: Elissa E
reviews.text:
Absolutely love this place.
Avo toast is delicious, staff are very attentive and kind. Coffee is great. Only wish they had almond milk 🙏

reviews.author: Manyi Wong
reviews.text: Both batch brew and cap are top quality. Beans have a deep and distinct flavour. Owner/barista is unaffectedly good, can feel his passion in his work. Just good coffee made well. Regret that it took me so long to try this place.

reviews.author: Georgie Zee
reviews.text: I bought a "long black" over the weekend just before the cafe closed and by the time I got to my car realised my cup was only half full. Flavor of coffee was great, very smooth and tasty. However $5.50 for a "long black" which only lasted a few sips, very disappointing.

reviews.author: Raymond Chan
reviews.text:
Stumbled across Susu Lane at night and it turned out to be a really nice surprise. The store has a warm, modern feel with wooden tones, soft lighting, and plenty of clean seating both inside and outside. It’s actually a lot more spacious than it looks from the street, and the layout makes it easy to sit down and relax without feeling crowded.

The shaved ice itself was the good but not mind blowing. The portion is okay, the texture is super fine, and the toppings have good variety. The one I tried had a refreshing fruity flavour with chewy pearls and jelly that balanced everything really well. You can definitely finish it as one person.

They also have a small range of cakes and tarts in the cabinet. Presentation was neat and the variety looked good, though some trays were already empty which probably means they sell out quickly.

Prices are around the mid range for shaved ice in Sydney.

reviews.author: Brittney
reviews.text: I purchased the original and mango tiramisu, and both were absolutely amazing — 10/10. I also tried the strawberry cold foam matcha and the tiramisu latte, which were just as delicious. The café is in a great location, not too crowded, with good indoor seating and a small but nice outdoor area. Unfortunately they were out of stock of a few items i was hoping to try. Prices were fair and definitely worth it for the quality. Highly recommend this place, and I’d love to return to try the bingsu next time.

reviews.author: Thu Ho
reviews.text: Saw this place got some reviews on tiktok and wanted to try out. Ordered the strawberry foam matcha, it was nice, not too sweet, but could use a little bit more matcha. The tiramisu (original) is delicious, very rich with the coffee and cacso taste, the cream is smooth, not too fatty so it’s a balance combination. Very lovely staff I met this morning.
